proven.claim: Prove: if the repository check succeeds, the target console.log is reached.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1 – Precondition Propagation / P4 – Temporal Analysis
; Prove: if the repository check succeeds, the target console.log is reached.

;--- 1. Declarations -------------------------------------------------
(declare-const isGitRepo Bool)      ; result of diff.isGitRepo()
(declare-const exited   Bool)       ; true iff process.exit was called
(declare-const reached  Bool)       ; true iff the target console.log is executed

;--- 2. Transitions (code behaviour) ----------------------------------
; The function calls process.exit only when the repo check fails.
;   if (!isGitRepo) { exited = true } else { exited = false }
(assert (= exited (not isGitRepo)))

; The target line is executed only when we have NOT exited.
;   reached = not exited
(assert (= reached (not exited)))

;--- 3. Precondition for the proof ------------------------------------
; We are interested in the case where the repository check succeeds.
(assert isGitRepo)   ; precondition: the current directory *is* a git repo

;--- 4. Negated property (what we want to show cannot happen) -------
; Suppose the target line is **not** reached.
(assert (not reached))

;--- 5. Check ---------------------------------------------------------
(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat  → the target console.log is guaranteed to run

clause_history.clause:

; Prove: if the repository check succeeds, the target console.log is reached.

;--- 1. Declarations -------------------------------------------------

(assert (= exited (not isGitRepo)))

; The target line is executed only when we have NOT exited.
;   reached = not exited

;--- 3. Precondition for the proof ------------------------------------

(assert isGitRepo)   ; precondition: the current directory *is* a git repo

;--- 4. Negated property (what we want to show cannot happen) -------

;--- 5. Check ---------------------------------------------------------

; Expected result: unsat  → the target console.log is guaranteed to run
